title: The Tavern Mystery
description: A short adventure to test NPC interactions. Talk to the locals, gather clues, and find the hidden treasure.
author: Claude

tavern.name: The Dusty Tavern
tavern.description: A dimly lit tavern with creaking floorboards. The smell of ale and woodsmoke fills the air. A worn counter stretches along the back wall. A heavy door to the east leads to the cellar stairs.

alley.name: The Back Alley
alley.description: A narrow alley behind the tavern. Crates and barrels are stacked against the walls. Moonlight filters through the gap between buildings.

cellar_stairs.name: Cellar Stairs
cellar_stairs.description: A narrow stone staircase leading down. At the bottom, a heavy oak door blocks the way forward.

cellar.name: The Cellar
cellar.description: A damp, cold cellar. Cobwebs hang from the low ceiling. In the center of the room sits a small ornate chest, gleaming faintly in the dim light.

notice_board.name: notice board
notice_board.description: A wooden board pinned with faded notices.
notice_board.examine_text: Most notices are about missing cats and odd jobs. But one stands out — a golden family heirloom has gone missing, last seen locked away in the tavern cellar. Its rightful owner is desperate to have it returned.

ale.name: mug of ale
ale.description: A foaming mug of ale sitting on the counter.

crate.name: wooden crate
crate.description: A sturdy wooden crate. Something might be hidden behind it.
crate.examine_text: You peer behind the crate and notice scratch marks on the wall, as if something was dragged here recently.

old_key.name: rusty key
old_key.description: An old iron key, coated in rust but still solid.

cellar_door.name: cellar door
cellar_door.description: A heavy oak door with an iron lock. It looks sturdy.

treasure_chest.name: treasure chest
treasure_chest.description: A small wooden chest with ornate carvings. It's locked tight.
treasure_chest.examine_text: You examine the chest closely. Along the side you notice a small hidden latch. Pressing it should release the lock.

heirloom.name: golden heirloom
heirloom.description: A beautiful golden locket on a fine chain, resting inside the open chest.

puzzles.name: Unlock the Cellar
puzzles.description: Use the rusty key to open the cellar door.
puzzles.completion_text: The rusty key turns with a grinding sound. The cellar door swings open.
puzzles.lock_fail_text: The door is locked. You need a key.

puzzles.name: Recover the Heirloom
puzzles.win_text: You pick up the golden locket from the chest. The lost heirloom! The barkeep will be thrilled. You've solved the tavern mystery!

barkeep.name: Barkeep
barkeep.description: A burly man with a thick beard and kind eyes. He polishes a glass absentmindedly while watching the room.

dialogue.text: The barkeep nods at you. "Welcome, stranger! You look like someone who could use a drink... and maybe a job. What can I do for you?"

choices.text: I saw the notice about a lost heirloom
What's the story with the cellar?

dialogue.text: His eyes light up. "You saw that, did you? My grandmother's locket went missing weeks ago. I think the old caretaker, Morven, hid it in the cellar before he vanished."

choices.text: How do I get into the cellar?
I'll see what I can find

dialogue.text: "The cellar's been locked up for months. Nobody goes down there anymore." He lowers his voice. "Strange noises coming from down there lately, though."
He scratches his beard sheepishly. "I had an old iron key for the cellar, but I dropped it somewhere in the back alley. Haven't had time to look for it. You're welcome to search for it."

"Old Morven. He was the caretaker here for twenty years. Good worker, kept to himself. Then one night he just... disappeared." The barkeep shakes his head. "Shady fellow, if you ask me. But I don't think he meant any harm."

choices.text: I'll find the heirloom for you
How do I get into the cellar?

dialogue.text: He glances around nervously. "Scraping sounds. Whispers. The regulars say it's haunted." He forces a laugh. "Probably just rats, right?"
"Well, help yourself to the ale on the counter. On the house." He nods toward the notice board. "And check the board if you're looking for work."

cat.name: Tabby Cat
cat.description: A scruffy orange tabby cat. It watches you with half-closed eyes, tail flicking lazily.

dialogue.text: The cat meows and rubs against your leg. It seems to want attention.
The cat purrs loudly and rolls onto its back. Then it suddenly jumps up and paws at the ground near the crates, meowing insistently. There seems to be something hidden there.
The cat hisses and darts behind the crates, knocking something loose. You hear a metallic clatter.

ghost.name: Ghostly Figure
ghost.description: A translucent, shimmering figure in old servant's clothes. It flickers in and out of visibility.

dialogue.text: The ghostly figure turns to face you. "So... someone finally came. I am Morven."
The ghost shakes its head sadly. "I didn't steal it — I was trying to protect it. Someone was going to take it from the tavern. I hid it in the chest so it would be safe."

choices.text: Who was trying to take it?
How do I open the chest?

dialogue.text: "I was the caretaker here for twenty years. When I discovered a thief planning to steal the locket, I hid it. But the effort... cost me everything." The figure flickers. "I've been trapped here ever since, waiting for someone to return it to its rightful owner."

choices.text: I'll return it. How do I open the chest?
Rest easy, Morven. I'll take care of it.

dialogue.text: "The chest holds the heirloom. Look at it carefully — there's a hidden latch on the side. Press it and the lock will open." The ghost smiles faintly. "Take the locket back upstairs to the barkeep. My task here will finally be done."
